name: Arby's

notes: Arby's current customer catalog was reconciled across ten owner-issued category pages plus the restaurant-linked July 2026 U.S. nutrition/allergen and ingredient guides. The canonical result contains 78 consumer products across 12 categories.

All 66 frozen rows were reconciled: 4 exact matches, 16 normalized product/size variants, and 46 ingredient-component, topping, or add-on artifacts. The 20 matched rows represented only 14 distinct current products; 64 current products were absent from the frozen output.

Twenty-five web presentations are configurable meal, kids-meal, or combo shells. Their underlying entree, side, and drink products are cataloged separately so an incomplete bundle-level allergen result is not presented as covering customer choices.

The current guide additionally publishes breakfast and optional/regional products even where no national web category page exists; these remain labeled in source-backed Breakfast and Optional & Regional categories.

Seventy-seven of 78 canonical products have current restaurant-issued row-level allergen evidence. Orange Cream Shake is published on the current website but absent from the July 2026 allergen guide, so its allergen outcome remains accurately unavailable.

Fixed Contains declarations are stored as allergens. The dagger common-fryer disclosure and star facility disclosure are stored separately as mayContain signals on 27 products; they are not promoted to fixed ingredients.
Eight matched frozen products lost current common-fryer or facility-contact signals even though their fixed allergen declarations were correct. The repaired data restores those signals.

The Alliance Kitchen sheet is restaurant-issued but explicitly limited to the shared kitchen at 1425 Ellsworth Industrial Boulevard NW in Atlanta. It is not evidence for national Arby's products or cross-contact conditions.

The reviewed extractor uses each category page's pre-rendered consumer names as the national catalog boundary, parses only product rows from the current nutrition/allergen guide, collapses size/count presentations to canonical products, and reads only the first two menu-assembly pages of the ingredient PDF.

The frozen extraction crossed the product/component boundary: it parsed page-three-and-later ingredient glossary entries, toppings, and sauce additions as products, then mixed location-specific Alliance Kitchen component evidence into the national restaurant.

Focused tests cover the 78-product contract, all 66 frozen dispositions, component-glossary exclusion, fixed-versus-contact semantics, current omissions, published repair invariants, target-only isolation, and idempotency.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Captured the owner menu landing page and ten current category pages, yielding 84 published presentations including 25 configurable bundle shells.

HTTP 200. Captured the current owner nutrition page and verified that it directly links the July 2026 nutrition/allergen and ingredient PDFs plus the March 2025 gluten guide and Alliance Kitchen sheet.

HTTP 200. Captured and visually reviewed all ten nutrition/allergen pages and all seven ingredient pages. The guide states that its information is effective July 2026 and explicitly distinguishes fixed allergens, common-fryer contact, facility processing, and optional/regional availability.
notes: The ingredient PDF's first two pages describe consumer product assembly; later pages define component ingredients and must not be emitted as products.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Visual review confirmed the sheet names Alliance Kitchen #9600 at 1425 Ellsworth Industrial Boulevard NW, Atlanta, Georgia and describes that shared kitchen's shellfish and tree-nut environment.
notes: This location-specific sheet was rejected as national product or cross-contact evidence.

findings.summary: The frozen catalog treated ingredient glossary entries, toppings, and sauce additions as consumer products, producing 46 non-product artifacts among 66 rows.
findings.resolution: Limited ingredient extraction to product assembly pages, used current web categories as the national product boundary, and removed every component/topping/add-on artifact.

findings.summary: The frozen output represented only 14 distinct current products and omitted 64 canonical products, including most sandwiches, chicken, sides, value items, breakfast, and regional products.
findings.resolution: Published the reconciled 78-product consumer catalog and documented 25 configurable bundle presentations separately.

findings.summary: The frozen output used a single Atlanta Alliance Kitchen shared-location sheet as evidence for national Arby's component rows.
findings.resolution: Removed Alliance Kitchen component rows and restricted national products to current U.S. guide and owner menu evidence.

findings.summary: Eight matched frozen products omitted current common-fryer or facility-processing signals, although their fixed Contains declarations matched.
findings.resolution: Restored row-specific fryer/facility signals as mayContain values while retaining fixed allergens separately.

findings.summary: Orange Cream Shake is published on the current owner menu but has no matching row in the current July 2026 allergen guide.
findings.resolution: Retained the current product with allergenSourceType unavailable and made no ingredient, negative, or cross-contact assumption.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt Arby's from current consumer menu categories and official July 2026 guides, removed component and location-scope corruption, restored omitted products, and separated fixed allergens from common-contact signals.
